mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns during rate cuts are statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.5 percent, labor market deterioration signals weakening consumer spending and corporate profit contraction, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.5 percent, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) after 2010.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate synchronized demand recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to generate positive returns in over 60 percent of co-occurring months, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month acceleration in the index of industrial production signals improving manufacturing capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days only during periods of stable inflation.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative returns in more than 50 percent of instances when CPIAUCSL growth exceeds 3 percent annually, or the 5-day return alpha vanishes (t-stat < 1.5) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the consumer price index raises inflation expectations and prompts market pricing of tighter monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in more than 55 percent of months with rising CPIAUCSL, or the rank correlation between CPI changes and forward returns remains below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 100 thousand persons indicates severe labor market contraction, which suppresses corporate earnings growth and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines by more than 100 thousand in a single month
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in over 60 percent of triggered months, or the 60-day forward return distribution shows no significant left skew (Jarque-Bera p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2 percent combined with rising real personal consumption expenditures confirms broad-based demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50 percent of quarters, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside a stable federal funds rate indicates supply-side recovery without monetary tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative returns in over 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS remains unchanged, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52 percent in walk-forward validation.
